item.title: Measles toll far higher than official count
item.description: The actual number of measles deaths and infections across Bangladesh is significantly higher than the figures officially disclosed by the Directorate General of Health Services as divisional data, hospital records and local health reports revealed......Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: How Middle East rewrites map of power
item.description: FOR decades, the United States sailed the oceans like an untouchable battleship, and Israel stood in the Middle East like a fortress wrapped in fire. Together, they shaped wars, toppled regimes, dictated oil routes, and drew red lines across deserts......

item.description: BANGLADESH’S rapid urbanisation has been a major driver of economic growth, but it has also created a range of complex urban challenges. Among them, the two major metropolitan cities, Dhaka and Chattogram, are now trapped in a triple crisis of......

item.description: SOCIAL mobility — often typified as upward, downward, inter-generational, intra-generational, horizontal and vertical — refers to the movement of individuals belonging to different strata in a society. It necessitates the understanding of changed......City & Local Guides

item.title: Silent public health threat
item.description: CAGES are stacked to the ceiling and birds crammed together in suffocating heat. Slaughter is carried out on wet floors and wastewater flows into open drains. Workers handle birds, knives and money in the same motion while customers stand inches......

item.description: AN ALMOST complete impasse in the 59 cases filed in connection with the BASIC Bank loan scam of Tk 22.65 billion is worrying. It has been 10 years since the cases were filed. The bank, once considered a sound state-owned financial institution, had......

item.title: Govt should strengthen cyber-crime response
item.description: CYBER crimes increase rapidly while the law enforcement agencies continue to struggle with inadequate logistical support, limited digital forensic capacity and a shortage of skilled investigators. Common offences include breaking into digital systems......

item.description: Vacuum cleaners and vapes could get more expensive if the Iran war drags on for much longer, Chinese factory owners and traders warn, as the world’s manufacturing hub reels from ‘crazy’ costs....

item.description: Across much of India, an energy crunch caused by the Iran war has prompted long queues for cooking gas cylinders. That’s not a problem for Gauri Devi....

item.description: China’s trade grew faster than expected last month, official data showed Saturday, withstanding pressure from war in the Middle East and reversing a decline in exports to the United States....

item.description: The parent company of Truth Social, president Donald Trump’s social media platform, reported a net loss on Friday of more than $400 million in the first quarter — mostly from tumbling cryptocurrency valuations....

item.description: An elderly man was allegedly tied to a tree and tortured publicly at Lalmonirhat sadar upazila over a dispute involving a migrant worker in Libya, sparking outrage after a video of the incident went viral on social media....

item.title: No compromise in trial of Uttara perpetrators: ICT chief prosecutor
item.description: The chief prosecutor of the International Crimes Tribunal, Md Aminul Islam, said on Saturday that there would be no compromise or bias in bringing to justice those responsible for the killings in the capital’s Uttara area during the 2024 mass uprising....

item.title: National body for constitutional reforms demanded
item.description: Speakers at a discussion on Saturday expressed concern over the fate of the 2026 referendum result and the July National Charter, as the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government preferred the amendment to the constitution rather than its reform....

item.description: A cruise ship hit with a deadly hantavirus outbreak is headed for Spain’s Canary Islands, where most of the nearly 150 people on board will be evacuated and flown home after weeks at sea....

item.description: Russian president Vladimir Putin said Saturday that his soldiers in Ukraine were fighting an ‘aggressive force’ backed by all of NATO and described his war goals as ‘just’, as he addressed a scaled back Victory Day parade on Red Square....

item.description: A teenage girl allegedly hanged herself at Chatmohar in Pabna after her employers brutally tortured and humiliated her over theft allegation....

item.description: The new government should modify the energy structure to keep plunderers at bay and maintain the country’s energy security, said experts at a seminar on Saturday in the capital....

item.description: Researchers, activists and families of martyrs of the War of Independence on Saturday said that the loss of the sole breadwinner in 1971 forced many low-income families into severe hardship after the country’s liberation....

item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam on Saturday said that the government would continue supporting cable operators in resolving their logical problems as the cable, ISP and broadcasting sectors were linked to the employment of millions of people across the country....

item.description: A seven-day children’s art exhibition, titled ‘Dolan Art Exhibition 2026’ and themed ‘Colourful Dream’, concluded on Saturday at Zainul Gallery, Dhaka University....

item.title: 19-day-old boy infected with measles
item.description: A 19-day-old baby boy was diagnosed with measles in Bangladesh on Tuesday, believed to be the youngest recorded measles patient in the country, amid an outbreak of the highly contagious disease that killed more than 350 children and infected over 50,000 people until Saturday from March 15....

item.description: Four-day Police Week 2026 is going to begin today during when the force will present six agendas to the prime minister, including the establishment of a cybercrime unit for the Bangladesh Police to ensure cyber security and prevent cybercrimes across the country....

item.title: Political, business influences weaken press freedom: confce
item.description: Journalists, editors and governance experts on Saturday said that increasingpoliticalcontrol over state institutions, the growing influence of business interests in politics and the rise of majoritarianism were weakening press freedom and democratic accountability in Bangladesh and across South Asia....

item.description: A group of students of Eden Mohila College broke the main gate of the institution on Saturday night demanding a ban on student politics on the campus....

item.description: Iran questioned the seriousness of American diplomacy on Saturday in the wake of renewed naval clashes in the Gulf, while keeping Washington waiting for a response to its latest negotiating position as Israeli airstrikes killed at least eight people in......

item.description: Three hikers — two Singaporeans and an Indonesian — died in an eruption Friday of Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano where they found themselves in a no-go zone, officials said......
